violations.description: Date marking and disposition of ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food. — --Refrigerated, ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food * (prepared more than 24 hours ago) was not properly date marked in walk-in-cooler.

Nonfood-contact surfaces - Design and construction. — --Nonfood contact surface was not designed and/or constructed to allow easy cleaning and to facilitate maintenance. Aluminum foil wrapped on shelves in kitchen area needs to be removed. Seals on 2 door prep cooler needs replaced.
Cleaning frequency of equipment and utensils. — --Food contact surface was not cleaned as frequently as required. Inside prep cooler needs cleaned of food debris.
Handwashing lavatories. — --Handwashing lavatory in the front was used for purpose other than handwashing. Rasberries being stored in sink. Remove.
Cleaning and maintenance of floors, walls and ceilings. — --Physical facilities (e.g. floors, walls, ceiling etc…)* were not clean or not cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean.

Preventing contamination during storage. — --Food item was not stored in a clean, dry location a minimum of 6 inches above the floor. Boxes of cut yellow unfried chips were stored on the floor by the back door.

Preventing contamination of packaged and unpackaged food. — --Different species of raw animal food was not properly separated from each other or arranged according to minimum cooking temperatures. Tubes of raw ground beef were stored in the same tub as raw whole beef cuts in the walk in cooler.

Mechanical cleaning and sanitizing requirements. — --Sanitize solution for mechanical warewashing machine was not maintained at the proper concentration. The dishwasher tested between 10 and 25 ppm for chlorine. Ran the dish washer four times while priming the sanitizer. Got the same result after the last run. This needs to be set at between 50 and 100 ppm chlorine.

Plumbing system. — --Plumbing system was not designed, constructed, operated and/or maintained according to regulation. The drain line for the walk in cooler cooling unit ran to a bucket on the floor. The drain line for the front ice bin in the dining area runs to a container under it and not a drain line.

Handwashing lavatories. — --Handwashing lavatory was not provided in sufficient number and/or not accessible to allow convenient use by employees. Parts for the grater were placed in the hand sink in the kitchen.

Design and construction of floors, walls, and ceilings. — --Floors, walls, and/or ceiling were not constructed or designed to be smooth, nonabsorbent, and easily cleanable. The wall under the stainless steel counter in the dining service area is bare sheet rock. This is not a cleanable surface.

Lighting. — --Lighting did not have protective shielding in areas where there is exposed food, clean equipment/utensils, or unwrapped single-service items. The light over the dry storage area by the walk in cooler was missing the shield.
Installed, maintained (1 pt deducted)
Clean, employee dressing area, cleaning equipment storage, living/sleeping quarters separate (1 pt deducted)
Provided, maintained (3 pts deducted)
Handwashing, good hygienic practices (5 pts deducted)
Method, temperature and time (5 pts deducted)
Provided as required, designed, constructed, installed, maintained, and cleaned (1 pt deducted)
